name: Improper Access Control for Register Interface
description: The product uses memory-mapped I/O registers that act as an interface to hardware functionality from software, but there is improper access control to those registers.
extended_description: Software commonly accesses peripherals in a System-on-Chip (SoC) or other device through a memory-mapped register interface. Malicious software could tamper with any security-critical hardware data that is accessible directly or indirectly through the register interface, which could lead to a loss of confidentiality and integrity.

mapping.rationale: This CWE entry is at the Base level of abstraction, which is a preferred level of abstraction for mapping to the root causes of vulnerabilities.

mechanism.root_cause_summary: The product uses memory-mapped I/O registers that act as an interface to hardware functionality from software, but there is improper access control to those registers.
typical_preconditions: untrusted or insufficiently validated state reaches sensitive operation
typical_sources: untrusted or insufficiently validated state reaches sensitive operation
negative_nonfix_signals: style refactor without changing the sensitive path

guard_patterns: same logical object and same sink context

recommended_guard_templates: same logical object and same sink context

stage1.goal: Classify patch chunks by how directly they repair the CWE root cause.
must_focus: direct change to vulnerable sink or validation site
changes that alter boundary, lifecycle, policy, or authorization relation
supporting helper or error-propagation logic needed by the main fix
must_avoid: generic logging changes without security semantics
refactors unrelated to the sensitive sink/path

stage2.goal: Construct discriminative root cause, anchor, predicates, and guards.
must_focus: anchor at the sink or dominant validation block
prefer predicates that encode relations, not lone generic tokens
choose function-local or anchor-window scope whenever possible
must_avoid: predicates that rely on generic names alone
anchors in wrappers that lack the core vulnerability mechanism
recommended_queries: same logical object and same sink context

stage3.goal: Verify whether the target tag still exhibits the vulnerable relation or already contains the fix relation.
must_focus: whether the sensitive sink/path still exists
whether the fix relation is locally present in the same code region
whether anchor relocation is required before concluding absence
must_avoid: declaring NOT_AFFECTED from generic token absence alone
using broad token matches as final evidence

bad_examples.summary: The register interface provides software access to hardware functionality. This functionality is an attack surface. This attack surface may be used to run...
bad_examples.snippet: Cryptographic key material stored in registers inside the cryptographic accelerator can be accessed by software.

bad_examples.summary: The example code is taken from the Control/Status Register (CSR) module inside the processor core of the HACK@DAC'19 buggy CVA6 SoC [REF-1340]. In RISC-V ISA...
bad_examples.snippet: if (csr_we || csr_read) begin if ((riscv::priv_lvl_t'(priv_lvl_o & csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl) != csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl) && !(csr_addr.address==riscv::CSR_MEPC)) begin csr_exception_o.cause =...

good_examples.summary: The register interface provides software access to hardware functionality. This functionality is an attack surface. This attack surface may be used to run...
good_examples.snippet: Key material stored in registers should never be accessible to software. Even if software can provide a key, all read-back paths to software should be disabled.

good_examples.summary: The example code is taken from the Control/Status Register (CSR) module inside the processor core of the HACK@DAC'19 buggy CVA6 SoC [REF-1340]. In RISC-V ISA...
good_examples.snippet: if (csr_we || csr_read) begin if ((riscv::priv_lvl_t'(priv_lvl_o & csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl) != csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl)) begin csr_exception_o.cause = riscv::ILLEGAL_INSTR; csr_exception_o.valid = 1'b1; end //...

observed_examples.description: virtualization product does not restrict access to debug and other processor registers in the hardware, allowing a crash of the host or guest OS
virtual interrupt controller in a virtualization product allows crash of host by writing a certain invalid value to a register, which triggers a fatal error instead of returning an error code
Driver exposes access to Model Specific Register (MSR) registers, allowing admin privileges.
Virtualization product does not restrict access to PCI command registers, allowing host crash from the guest.
